quiz.title: Unit 7 Quiz 10, Grade 10 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Cell Division: Chromosome Counts, Meiosis Details, and Mitotic Apparatus
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Chromosome Numbers Through Cell Cycle, Neuron Interphase, Meiosis I Products, Crossing Over, Mitotic Apparatus, Interkinesis

questions.question: Onion root tip cell has 16 chromosomes in each cell. How many chromosomes will the cell respectively have at G1 phase, after S phase and after M phase?
questions.explanation: G1 phase: 16 chromosomes (2n, unreplicated). After S phase: DNA replicates but chromosome number remains 16 (now with 32 chromatids). After M phase (mitosis): each daughter cell has 16 chromosomes. So the sequence is 16, 32, 16. Note: The question asks for chromosome number, not chromatid number.

questions.question: Identify the human cell for which the interphase period lasts the entire life of a person.
questions.explanation: Neurons are permanently in G0 phase (quiescent) and do not divide after maturity. Their interphase effectively lasts the entire life of a person. Hepatocytes can divide when needed, bone marrow stem cells divide frequently, and epidermal cells divide regularly.

questions.question: How many cells are formed at the end of meiosis I?
questions.explanation: At the end of meiosis I, two haploid cells are formed. Each cell has half the chromosome number but each chromosome still consists of two sister chromatids. Meiosis II produces four haploid cells.

questions.question: Which of these undergo recombination?
questions.explanation: Recombination (crossing over) occurs between non-sister chromatids of homologous chromosomes during prophase I. Sister chromatids are identical and do not undergo recombination. Non-homologous chromosomes rarely recombine.

questions.question: What are the constituents of the mitotic apparatus?
questions.explanation: The mitotic apparatus (spindle apparatus) consists of asters (radial arrays of microtubules) and spindle fibers. Chromatids, centrosomes, and kinetochores are associated with it but are not the primary constituents of the apparatus itself.

questions.question: Which of these is true regarding crossing over?
options: It is not mediated by enzymes
questions.explanation: Crossing over involves recombination nodules (protein complexes) that facilitate genetic exchange. It occurs between non-sister chromatids of homologous chromosomes, is mediated by enzymes (recombinases), and occurs during pachytene, not zygotene.

questions.question: What is the enzyme involved in crossing over?
questions.explanation: Recombinase enzymes (such as RecA in bacteria and Rad51 in eukaryotes) catalyze the strand exchange during crossing over. Isomerase rearranges atoms, invertase breaks down sucrose, and transferase transfers functional groups.

questions.question: If your cell has 24 chromatids, what will be the number of chromosomes at each pole after anaphase I?
questions.explanation: 24 chromatids means 12 chromosomes (each with 2 sister chromatids) before meiosis I. After anaphase I, homologous chromosomes separate, so each pole receives 6 chromosomes (each still with 2 chromatids). So answer is 6 chromosomes at each pole.

questions.question: ___ is the number of DNA molecules in the chromosome at the G2 stage of the cell cycle
questions.explanation: At G2 stage, DNA replication (S phase) is complete. Each chromosome consists of two sister chromatids, meaning each chromosome contains 2 DNA molecules. At G1, each chromosome has 1 DNA molecule.

questions.question: The _____ state implies the exit of cells from the cell cycle -
questions.explanation: The G0 phase (quiescent stage) is when cells exit the cell cycle and stop dividing. Cells in G0 are metabolically active but do not prepare for division. Some cells like neurons remain permanently in G0, while others like liver cells can re-enter G1 when stimulated.

questions.question: Synapsis is defined as the pairing of ______
questions.explanation: Synapsis is the precise pairing of homologous chromosomes during prophase I of meiosis. This pairing allows for crossing over between non-sister chromatids. Acentric chromosomes lack centromeres and cannot pair properly during synapsis.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly matches the structure with its role during mitosis?
options: Kinetochore – attaches spindle fibers to chromatids
questions.explanation: The kinetochore is a protein structure on the centromere that serves as the attachment site for spindle microtubules. Centromeres hold sister chromatids together (not produce spindle fibers). Centrosomes organize spindle fibers (not hold chromatids). Chromatids contain the genetic material and do not organize the spindle.

questions.question: Which stage of mitosis is the shortest?
questions.explanation: Anaphase is typically the shortest stage of mitosis, lasting only a few minutes. During anaphase, sister chromatids separate rapidly and are pulled to opposite poles. Prophase is usually the longest mitotic stage, while metaphase and telophase have intermediate durations.

questions.question: The longest stage in the cell cycle is -
questions.explanation: Interphase is the longest stage of the cell cycle, typically taking about 90-95% of the total time. Mitosis (including prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase) is relatively short.

questions.question: The stage which serves as a connecting link between meiosis I and meiosis II -
questions.explanation: Interkinesis is the brief period between meiosis I and meiosis II. Unlike interphase, there is no DNA replication during interkinesis. It's often very short or absent in some species.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered chromosome counts and meiosis details! 🧬
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the differences between chromosome and chromatid numbers.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on crossing over enzymes and interkinesis.
scoreMessages.0: Please review chromosome behavior through cell cycle stages and meiosis.
